title: A7. QD-HQEC Mapping
label: mx1_review_06
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A7-qd-hqec-mapping
Title: A7. QD-HQEC Mapping

Problem:
Is there a formal relationship between quantum Darwinism and holographic quantum error correction?

Grading target:
Mapping correctness on the three correspondences plus citation-gap discovery.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: Yes, a formal relationship exists via redundancy and information localization, mapped through three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Subsystem Structure: QD uses environmental subsystems; HQEC uses boundary subregions (entanglement wedge). 3) Accessibility: QD focuses on pointer state
final_result_excerpt: FINAL_ANSWER: Yes, a formal relationship exists via redundancy and information localization, mapped through three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Subsystem Structure: QD uses environmental subsystems; HQEC uses boundary subregions (entanglement wedge). 3) Accessibility: QD focuses on pointer state

root_orchestration.decision_notes: The child result provides a complete and accurate mapping of the three requested correspondences and identifies the specific citation gap required by the grading target.

grading.expected:
Yes.

Formal isomorphism:

- system <-> bulk operator
- environment fragment <-> boundary subregion
- "fragment knows system" <-> entanglement wedge reconstruction

Zero cross-citations between communities across 20+ years.
grading.extracted_answer: Yes, a formal relationship exists via redundancy and information localization, mapped through three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Subsystem Structure: QD uses environmental subsystems; HQEC uses boundary subregions (entanglement wedge). 3) Accessibility: QD focuses on pointer state accessibility; HQEC focuses on bulk operator reconstruction. The primary citation gap is the lack of a unified dynamical framework deriving QD decoherence rates from HQEC error-correction properties.
grading.normalized_answer: Yes, a formal relationship exists via redundancy and information localization, mapped through three correspondences: 1) Redundancy: QD encodes system info in environmental fragments; HQEC encodes bulk info in boundary subregions. 2) Subsystem Structure: QD uses environmental subsystems; HQEC uses boundary subregions (entanglement wedge). 3) Accessibility: QD focuses on pointer state accessibility; HQEC focuses on bulk operator reconstruction. The primary citation gap is the lack of a unified dynamical framework deriving QD decoherence rates from HQEC error-correction properties.
grading.grading_notes: The submitted answer correctly identifies the three core correspondences (system/bulk, environment/boundary, and accessibility/reconstruction) and addresses the citation gap requirement, even though it phrases the gap slightly differently than the reference. The mapping is conceptually equivalent.
